query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(360012, 'What does "theology proper" specifically refer to?', 'multiple_choice', '["The study of Christ","The doctrine of God","The study of salvation","The doctrine of the church"]', 'The doctrine of God', 1),
(360012, 'The Hebrew term Elohim in Genesis 1:1 emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["God''s weakness","God''s unrivaled supremacy in creation","Human potential","Angelic beings"]', 'God''s unrivaled supremacy in creation', 2),
(360012, 'What does YHWH (the tetragrammaton) reveal about God?', 'multiple_choice', '["His created nature","His personal and covenantal nature","His limitations","His dependence on creation"]', 'His personal and covenantal nature', 3),
(360012, 'In Exodus 34:6-7, God is described as:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distant and uncaring","Merciful, gracious, and abounding in steadfast love","Unpredictable and capricious","Limited in power"]', 'Merciful, gracious, and abounding in steadfast love', 4),
(360012, 'Isaiah 6:1-8 primarily emphasizes which attribute of God?', 'multiple_choice', '["God''s weakness","God''s holiness and majesty","God''s need for human help","God''s limitations"]', 'God''s holiness and majesty', 5),
(360012, 'The Shema (Deuteronomy 6:4) affirms:', 'multiple_choice', '["Polytheism","Israel''s monotheistic confession","The existence of many gods","Human autonomy"]', 'Israel''s monotheistic confession', 6),
(360012, 'John Calvin''s theology emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Human autonomy","God''s sovereignty and providence","Rejection of Scripture","Salvation by works"]', 'God''s sovereignty and providence', 7),
(360012, 'Thomas Aquinas articulated God''s attributes through:', 'multiple_choice', '["Rejection of reason","The via negativa and via eminentiae","Pure speculation","Denial of mystery"]', 'The via negativa and via eminentiae', 8),
(360012, 'The Greek term kyrios applied to Jesus affirms:', 'multiple_choice', '["His humanity only","His divine authority and lordship","His limitations","His created nature"]', 'His divine authority and lordship', 9),
(360012, 'Why is a robust doctrine of God indispensable for pastoral ministry?', 'multiple_choice', '["It is merely academic","It shapes worship, ethics, and pastoral care","It replaces practical ministry","It is optional for believers"]', 'It shapes worship, ethics, and pastoral care', 10),
(360012, 'Discuss how the doctrine of God serves as the central axis around which all other theological loci revolve.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The doctrine of God provides the foundation for understanding Christology, pneumatology, soteriology, and all other doctrines', 11);

(360012, 'Discuss how the doctrine of God serves as the central axis around which all other theological loci revolve.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The doctrine of God provides the foundation for understanding Christology, pneumatology, soteriology, and all other doctrines', 11);
